news.title: Proptimes closes FY26 with over ₹61 crore revenue, sets ₹100 crore target for FY27
news.para: Proptimes Consultancy Services Pvt. Ltd. has crossed a significant revenue milestone of over Rs 61 crore for FY 2025-26. The real estate consulting firm is now aiming for over Rs 100 crore in revenue for FY 2026-27. Proptimes has expanded its operations to the UAE. The company is focused on growth through technology and partnerships with developers.

news.title: ED raids seven premises linked to Kolkata-based Merlin Group
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has raided the properties linked to the Merlin Group in Kolkata and its leaders, intensifying its probe into a money laundering case. The agency has alleged that false land titles were created using forged paperwork, prompting serious claims of land theft.

news.title: Haryana RERA's Gurugram bench clears all pending cases till 2024
news.para: HRERA Gurugram has made remarkable progress by clearing its backlog and resolving all complaints lodged until 2024, which included a staggering 2,100 cases. Last year, they disposed of 5,024 complaints, showcasing their commitment to prompt justice.

news.title: Square Yards ties up with RBI Innovation Hub to offer property data services via ULI
news.para: Square Yards is collaborating with RBIH to bring digital property valuation and title search services to financial institutions. This will be done through the Unified Lending Interface platform. This initiative aims to simplify digital lending processes by providing lenders with structured data on property transactions and ownership.

news.title: Kolhapur civic action committee raises concerns over third development plan draft
news.para: Kolhapur's civic action committee met town planning officials, alleging the third development plan prioritizes reservations over essential city amenities. They highlighted a decrease in gardens, schools, and parking despite population growth. Officials assured that suggestions would be considered for sector-wise implementation.

news.title: The next billion learners: How India can lead the global education economy
news.para: The Annual Education Summit 2026, scheduled for 11–12 June 2026 at the Yashobhoomi Convention Centre, New Delhi, comes at a critical juncture in this evolution. The education summit will serve as a confluence of ideas and action, where conversations around AI in education, global collaborations, skilling, and digital transformation move beyond theory to implementation.

news.title: Ranchi civic body targets illegal commercial enterprises in residential areas
news.para: The Ranchi Municipal Corporation is cracking down on illegal commercial establishments operating in residential Jharkhand State Housing Board colonies. Enforcement teams are conducting door-to-door surveys and cancelling trade licenses in areas like Harmu and Bariatu, following Supreme Court norms on land use. This action aims to prevent residential areas from becoming commercial hubs, disturbing civic planning and infrastructure.

news.title: Mangaluru civic body plans construction & demolition waste unit at Pachanady
news.para: Mangaluru City Corporation is planning to reserve land at Pachanady for scientific management of construction and demolition waste. Builders will be charged a fee to use this facility, and hefty penalties will be imposed on those caught dumping waste in public places or near rivers. This move aims to curb rampant illegal dumping.

news.title: RBI keeps policy rate unchanged at 5.25% amid inflationary pressure
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has chosen to hold its key interest rate at 5.25 percent, a step that reflects a cautious optimism amidst improving global economic sentiments following a ceasefire in the US-Iran tensions. This policy decision is crucial for India's import-driven market, as it underscores a commitment to managing inflation in line with the government's fresh targets.

news.title: Prestige Estates' pre-sales rose 76% to hit record ₹30,024 crore in FY26
news.para: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd. reported a 10% rise in Q4 pre-sales to Rs 7,697 crore, driven by strong housing property demand. The company achieved a record Rs 30,024 crore in sales bookings for FY26, a 76% increase year-on-year. This growth reflects consistent sales momentum and resilient end-user demand across key markets.

news.title: Madhya Pradesh cabinet approves ₹590 crore for a new airstrip in Ujjain
news.para: Madhya Pradesh cabinet approved Rs 590 crore for a new airstrip in Ujjain ahead of the 2028 Simhastha Kumbh Mela. The state also cleared over Rs 15,000 crore for irrigation, education, and forest conservation projects. The Katna Micro Irrigation Project in Mandsaur district received Rs 88.41 crore. The fee reimbursement scheme and PM Shri School scheme will continue.

news.title: NGT refuses to stay Telangana government's Future City project, calls for counters
news.para: The National Green Tribunal's southern zone bench refused to halt the Telangana government's Future City Project, directing authorities to file responses. An environmentalist had petitioned, alleging development began without mandatory environmental clearances. The tribunal adjourned the case for further hearing on June 9.

news.title: Haryana: After HC stay on S+4, town planning asks HSVP how infrastructure funds were used
news.para: Haryana has paused all stilt-plus-four construction approvals. This follows a court stay and concerns over infrastructure capacity. The town and country planning department is now demanding a report on how 689 crore rupees were spent on upgrades. This money was meant for infrastructure linked to increased density. The court highlighted the strain on services like sewage and traffic.

news.title: Ghaziabad: GDA makes rooftop solar panels space in building maps must for approval
news.para: Ghaziabad mandates rooftop solar panel space in building maps, rejecting non-compliant submissions. A refundable fee, tiered by plot size, is required for map approval under the PM Surya Ghar scheme. This initiative aims to make Ghaziabad a solar city, targeting 10% renewable energy by 2027.

news.title: Mumbai: BMC to auction prime Worli plot at ₹455 crore
news.para: Weeks after an inquiry was ordered into a Rs 600-crore tender, the BMC has now floated a new tender to auction a prime 5,166sqm plot for commercial exploitation at a base price of Rs 455 crore. The highest bidder can construct residential or commercial towers, with the developer also required to provide office space for the BMC's testing laboratory.

news.title: Mumbai developers pour ₹11,626 crore into BMC coffers in FY26
news.para: Developers emerged as the BMC's top revenue source in 2025-26, contributing Rs 11,626 crore through premiums and development charges, significantly exceeding property tax collections. This surge is attributed to extensive construction, particularly building redevelopment, with areas like Bandra-Jogeshwari leading revenue generation.

news.title: CCI clears Indriya's 14.3% stake acquisition in Aditya Birla Housing Finance
news.para: The Competition Commission of India has officially sanctioned Indriya's foray into Aditya Birla Housing Finance, paving the way for a landmark ₹2,750 crore investment. Through this deal, Indriya, already a part of Advent International, will capture a 14.29% ownership, leaving Aditya Birla Capital firmly in control of the majority stake in its housing finance operations.

news.title: Embassy REIT approves ₹1,100 crore commercial paper issuance
news.para: Embassy REIT is set to secure ₹1,100 crore via commercial papers, a strategic initiative aimed at refinancing existing obligations and bolstering working capital. This issuance will be executed in two batches, with durations of 347 and 342 days, forming a crucial segment of their ambitious ₹10,500 crore fundraising goal.

news.title: CCI clears Warburg Pincus' stake buy in Fleur Hotels; approves Lemon Tree restructuring
news.para: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has officially sanctioned Warburg Pincus' stake acquisition in Fleur Hotels, part of the Lemon Tree Hotels umbrella. With this nod of approval, the Lemon Tree group is set for an internal transformation, fostering two distinct avenues for expansion.

news.title: Government to soon restore conversion of DDA properties from leasehold to freehold: Housing minister
news.para: Property owners in Delhi can soon convert their leasehold properties to freehold. Union minister Manohar Lal stated that conversion rates are being finalized. This will allow the process to resume after a halt. New circle rates announced by the Delhi government will be applicable.
